report_name: Minnesota's Winona County Suffers Second Crippling Ransomware Attack This Year

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Cybercriminal Group (April 2026)
threat_actors.description: An unnamed cybercriminal group responsible for the April 7, 2026 ransomware attack against Winona County, Minnesota. A preliminary investigation suggests this group is distinct from the one that attacked the county in January 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The attackers deployed ransomware to encrypt files on Winona County's network, causing a debilitating disruption of government services.

attack_patterns.name: Service Stop
attack_patterns.description: Critical government services, such as the DMV and Vital Statistics, were stopped as a direct result of the ransomware attack and subsequent containment efforts.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely attempted to inhibit system recovery by deleting volume shadow copies using 'wmic.exe shadowcopy delete', as indicated by cyber observables for detection.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely used encoded PowerShell commands to download tools or execute malicious code, a common precursor to ransomware deployment.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The attackers may have used remote administration tools like PsExec for lateral movement across the county's network.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely used tools like Mimikatz to harvest credentials to facilitate lateral movement and escalate privileges within the network.

attack_patterns.name: Create Account
attack_patterns.description: The attackers may have created new user accounts, particularly those with administrative privileges, to establish persistence on the network, as indicated by monitoring for Windows Event ID 4720.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: The remote administration tool PsExec was listed as an observable for detecting lateral movement across the network.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: The credential dumping tool Mimikatz was listed as a file-based observable for detection.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The WMI command-line utility (wmic.exe) was observed being used with 'shadowcopy delete' arguments to inhibit system recovery.

identities.name: Winona County Government
identities.description: The local government for Winona County, Minnesota, which was the victim of a debilitating ransomware attack in April 2026, its second of the year.

identities.name: Minnesota National Guard
identities.description: The state's military reserve force, whose cyber protection team was deployed by the governor to assist Winona County with incident response and recovery.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
identities.description: The U.S. federal law enforcement agency involved in the criminal investigation of the ransomware attack on Winona County.

indicators.name: Encoded PowerShell Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Encoded PowerShell Command Execution
id: 9c5b1f3a-1d2e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of encoded PowerShell commands, often used by attackers to obfuscate malicious code.
references:
  - https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/minnesota-national-guard-deployed-to-help-winona-county-after-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of encoded PowerShell commands, a technique frequently used by attackers to download tools or execute malicious code, as mentioned in the report's cyber observables.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution For Lateral Movement
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: PsExec Execution
id: a8b7c6d5-e4f3-4a2b-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a remote administration tool often abused for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/minnesota-national-guard-deployed-to-help-winona-county-after-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec.exe, a tool mentioned as an observable for detecting lateral movement.

indicators.name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
id: f1e2d3c4-b5a6-4987-9654-3210fedcba98
status: experimental
description: Detects attempts to delete volume shadow copies using wmic.exe, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/minnesota-national-guard-deployed-to-help-winona-county-after-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'wmic.exe shadowcopy delete' command, which was listed as a key observable for detecting attempts to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 1234abcd-efgh-4ijk-lmno-pqrstuvwxyz01
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of mimikatz.exe, a tool used for credential dumping.
references:
  - https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/minnesota-national-guard-deployed-to-help-winona-county-after-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of mimikatz.exe, a credential dumping tool listed as a cyber observable.

indicators.name: New User Account Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: New User Account Created
id: b8d58b4a-29ef-4e64-bb7c-215ffff2236e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new user account (Windows Event ID 4720), which can be a sign of persistence.
references:
  - https://www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/news/minnesota-national-guard-deployed-to-help-winona-county-after-cyberattack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1136
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: security
detection:
  selection:
    EventID: 4720
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a new user account (Windows Event ID 4720), which was noted as an observable for detecting persistence.

relationships.source_name: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
